title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Authorization, Ethics, Privacy, and Evidence · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in authorized stakeholder and evidence charter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Define the stakeholder decision, users, page population, evidence, success, failure, review cadence, and observable value before choosing crawler technology.
Record who controls the target, what access is authorized, which identities and paths are permitted, and who can pause or revoke the crawl.
Separate technical policy from terms, contracts, copyright, privacy, and jurisdiction questions and route uncertain cases to qualified review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Boundaries
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-evidence-boundaries-2z3gbb" that uses case 9295 to gate the crawl charter on authorization, stakeholder value, minimized data, and honest evidence limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Boundaries.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Boundaries into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Boundaries, keep Evidence Boundaries connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-evidence-boundaries-2z3gbb
# Competency: scraper-evidence-boundaries.
# Case 9295: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def evidence_scraper_evidence_boundaries_2z3gbb(authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 8, minimized_fields: int = 6, retained_fields: int = 6, transfer_gates_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_2z3gbb = "2z3gbb-bbg3z2-9295"
    assert evidence_variant_2z3gbb
    evidence_axes_2z3gbb = (
        "authorization2z3gbb",
        "url9295",
        "responsebbg3z2",
        "tree2z3gbb9295",
        "frontier92952z3gbb",
        "reportbbg3z29295",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_2z3gbb)) == 6
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "authorization-missing"
    if acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "outcome-underdefined"
    if minimized_fields < 1 or retained_fields != minimized_fields:
        return False, retained_fields, "data-minimization"
    if not transfer_gates_named:
        return False, retained_fields, "evidence-overclaim"
    assert authorized and acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "authorized-crawl-charter"
